title: Complex vowels & checkpoint
pedagogy: Vowels (4/4): complex vowels ia (เ◌ีย), uea (เ◌ือ), ua (◌ัว), plus ai (ไ◌/ใ◌), ao (เ◌า), am (◌ำ) — the four written-short vowels that count as LIVE for tone rules. Vowel checkpoint.

exercises.title: Vowels built from pieces
exercises.thaiExample: เ◌ีย เ◌ือ ◌ัว
body: Complex vowels glue several marks around one consonant: เ◌ีย (ia), เ◌ือ (uea), ◌ัว (ua). Read each as a single unit — เกีย is k + ia, not four separate sounds.
Four more — ไ◌ ใ◌ เ◌า ◌ำ — are spoken short, but for tone purposes they count as LIVE syllables, because they end in a glide or an m.
That exception matters constantly: ไป, เขา, and น้ำ all behave as live syllables.
